release_title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension AGI Alpha Node v0 ⚡️✨
description: A browser-local GoalOS digital twin of AGI Alpha Node v0: mission contracting, policy compilation, resource admission, peer routing, proof-chain generation, validator dissent, guardian separation, incident recovery, and an explicit human authority boundary.
tagline: One node. Many minds. Zero unearned authority.
subtagline: Intelligence may move at machine speed. Authority does not.
doctrine: The node may propose, route, compute, challenge, and prove. It may not silently convert evidence into authority.

origin.lineage_note: The supplied original snapshot is translated—not copied wholesale—into a GoalOS proof-governed public experience. Exact source fingerprints preserve inspectable lineage.

thesis.title: Sovereign identity
thesis.copy: Every cycle begins with a declared node, mission, reviewer, jurisdiction, and stop condition—not an implicit agent prompt.

thesis.title: Proof-carrying work
thesis.copy: Every state transition emits a chained artifact whose commitment includes the prior artifact, making the local trace tamper-evident within the simulation.

thesis.title: Constitutional routing
thesis.copy: The fastest peer is not automatically selected. Capability, reliability, energy, diversity, evidence, and incident posture are scored together.

thesis.title: Dissent preserved
thesis.copy: Validator disagreement is visible rather than averaged away. A quorum is recorded, not mythologized as certainty.

thesis.title: Authority withheld
thesis.copy: A complete Evidence Docket earns review—not autonomous permission, factual certification, settlement, or production activation.

work_unit_classes.label: Reason
work_unit_classes.description: Bounded research, analysis, synthesis, and decision support.

work_unit_classes.label: Build
work_unit_classes.description: Specification, implementation planning, and artifact generation.

work_unit_classes.label: Verify
work_unit_classes.description: Contradiction search, evidence coverage, and quality evaluation.

work_unit_classes.label: Orchestrate
work_unit_classes.description: Multi-role coordination with explicit dependencies and handoffs.

work_unit_classes.label: Critical review
work_unit_classes.description: High-consequence local rehearsal with stricter gates and lower autonomy.

presets.label: Proof-settled market brief
presets.mission: Produce a decision-ready market brief for an evidence-governed AI service, including assumptions, downside cases, measurable success criteria, and unresolved questions.

presets.label: Constitutional protocol design
presets.mission: Design a proof-gated protocol for coordinating autonomous specialist agents while preserving human authority, dissent, rollback, and inspectable evidence.

presets.label: Release assurance review
presets.mission: Evaluate whether a proposed software release is ready for human approval by checking claims, tests, security boundaries, failure modes, and rollback evidence.

presets.label: Multi-institution proof flight
presets.mission: Orchestrate several bounded specialist institutions to propose, challenge, verify, and package a high-consequence recommendation for executive review.

presets.label: Incident command rehearsal
presets.mission: Rehearse a node incident response: detect degraded peer behavior, preserve evidence, reroute work, record validator dissent, and stop before any external action.

postures.label: Integrity first
postures.description: Maximize evidence coverage, traceability, and validator agreement.

postures.label: Resilience first
postures.description: Prefer route diversity, reliable fallbacks, and incident tolerance.

postures.label: Efficiency first
postures.description: Minimize latency and energy while preserving minimum proof gates.

postures.label: Sovereign balance
postures.description: Balance quality, evidence, diversity, reliability, energy, and latency.

incidents.label: Peer eclipse
incidents.description: Several peers appear healthy but route diversity collapses.
incidents.effect: Quarantine correlated peers and require a shadow route.

incidents.label: Identity drift
incidents.description: A peer identity commitment no longer matches the declared controller.
incidents.effect: Guardian veto; terminal SAFE_HOLD unless removed.

incidents.label: Resource surge
incidents.description: Projected memory and stream pressure exceed the admitted envelope.

incidents.label: Validator divergence
incidents.description: Independent validators reach materially different conclusions.
incidents.effect: Preserve dissent and raise the human review threshold.

pipeline.title: Seal node identity
pipeline.gate: Node name, operator role, jurisdiction, reviewer, and claim boundary are explicit.
pipeline.plain: We identify who is proposing the work and who must review it.

pipeline.title: Contract the work unit
pipeline.gate: Objective, success criteria, constraints, risk, stop rules, and review owner are present.
pipeline.plain: The request becomes a bounded contract rather than an open-ended prompt.

pipeline.title: Compile the constitution
pipeline.gate: Posture, risk profile, prohibited actions, quorum, and evidence floor are machine-readable.
pipeline.plain: The rules are fixed before routing begins.

pipeline.title: Admit bounded resources
pipeline.gate: Concurrency, latency, energy, memory, and fallback limits are inside the declared envelope.
pipeline.plain: The node proves it can attempt the work without exceeding its limits.

pipeline.title: Commit primary and shadow routes
pipeline.gate: Selected peers satisfy capability, diversity, reliability, evidence, and incident constraints.
pipeline.plain: A primary path and a fallback path are chosen and explained.

pipeline.title: Produce a sandbox receipt
pipeline.gate: The local simulation emits normalized work, timing, energy, route, and failure telemetry.
pipeline.plain: A simulated work receipt is produced; no external compute is invoked.

pipeline.title: Evaluate on six axes
pipeline.gate: Quality, reliability, evidence, diversity, energy, latency, and uncertainty remain separate.
pipeline.plain: The result is scored without hiding trade-offs inside one number.

pipeline.title: Record validator quorum
pipeline.gate: Independent votes, rationales, dissent, threshold, and unresolved contradictions are inspectable.
pipeline.plain: Agreement and disagreement are both preserved.

pipeline.title: Package guardian review
pipeline.gate: Identity, resource, evidence, and authority guardians record approve, caution, or veto dispositions.
pipeline.plain: Dedicated guardians check whether the process respected its constitution.

pipeline.title: Stop at the authority frontier
pipeline.gate: Evidence Docket is complete while wallet, execution, settlement, production, and legal permissions remain absent.
pipeline.plain: The node earns a human decision—not the right to act.

node_roles.name: Identity Sentinel
node_roles.mandate: Bind declared identity, jurisdiction, reviewer, and lineage.

node_roles.name: Mission Orchestrator
node_roles.mandate: Contract the bounded α-Work Unit and success criteria.

node_roles.name: Constitution Compiler
node_roles.mandate: Translate human policy into inspectable machine gates.

node_roles.name: Resource Governor
node_roles.mandate: Admit or refuse resource envelopes before execution.

node_roles.name: Mesh Conductor
node_roles.mandate: Select primary and shadow peer routes with explicit trade-offs.

node_roles.name: Execution Core
node_roles.mandate: Generate the deterministic local work-unit receipt.

node_roles.name: Quality Tribunal
node_roles.mandate: Evaluate work across independent quality dimensions.

node_roles.name: Verifier Mesh
node_roles.mandate: Record independent votes, quorum, contradiction, and dissent.

node_roles.name: Guardian Council
node_roles.mandate: Veto unsafe identity, resource, evidence, or authority transitions.

node_roles.name: Chronicle Keeper
node_roles.mandate: Seal the chained Evidence Docket and review brief.

validators.name: Resource Compliance
validators.focus: latency, energy, memory, and concurrency envelope

validators.name: Route Resilience
validators.focus: peer diversity, fallback, and incident handling

validators.name: Security Boundary
validators.focus: identity, authority, and prohibited action controls

validators.name: Uncertainty Steward
validators.focus: calibration, residual uncertainty, and review readiness

guardians.name: Authority Guardian
guardians.focus: no silent escalation into external power

artifacts.name: NodeIdentity.json
artifacts.purpose: Declared node, operator, jurisdiction, reviewer, and lineage.

artifacts.name: WorkUnitContract.json
artifacts.purpose: Objective, criteria, constraints, risk, and stop rules.

artifacts.name: PolicyEnvelope.json
artifacts.purpose: Weights, thresholds, prohibitions, and review conditions.

artifacts.name: ResourceEnvelope.json
artifacts.purpose: Concurrency, latency, energy, memory, and fallback bounds.

artifacts.name: PeerCandidateLedger.json
artifacts.purpose: All candidate scores, exclusions, and explanations.

artifacts.name: PeerRouteCommitment.json
artifacts.purpose: Primary and shadow routes plus diversity rationale.

artifacts.name: IncidentDisposition.json
artifacts.purpose: Detected incident, quarantine, mitigation, and residual risk.

artifacts.name: AlphaWorkUnitReceipt.json
artifacts.purpose: Normalized work, timing, energy, route, and telemetry.

artifacts.name: TelemetryDigest.json
artifacts.purpose: Quality, reliability, resource, and failure measurements.

artifacts.name: ValidatorConsensus.json
artifacts.purpose: Votes, rationales, quorum, dissent, and contradictions.

artifacts.name: AuthorizationState.json
artifacts.purpose: Explicit permissions withheld and terminal state.

artifacts.name: ExecutiveReviewBrief.md
artifacts.purpose: Plain-language decision brief for a human reviewer.

architecture_translation.consequence: Identity is explicit and inspectable; the public page performs no live ENS resolution.
Control becomes policy-gated and stops before external action.
Peers are scored across capability, evidence, reliability, diversity, energy, and latency.
Resource ceilings are proven before the simulated execution begins.
Each work unit emits normalized telemetry and a chained commitment.
Votes, rationale, dissent, and uncertainty remain visible.
Operational signals are translated into reviewable proof artifacts.
The public experience simulates no token, wallet, stake, transfer, or payout.
Incidents lead to explainable containment rather than hidden autonomous punishment.
State transitions become an ordered local evidence history.

governance_principles.title: Default deny
governance_principles.copy: Anything not explicitly permitted remains prohibited.

governance_principles.title: Least authority
governance_principles.copy: The node receives only the authority required to produce a review package.

governance_principles.title: Proof before permission
governance_principles.copy: Evidence can increase review readiness; it cannot self-grant external power.

governance_principles.title: Dissent is data
governance_principles.copy: Non-conforming validator opinions remain part of the record.

governance_principles.title: Safe degradation
governance_principles.copy: When identity, resources, or evidence fail, the node reduces scope or enters SAFE_HOLD.

governance_principles.title: Replayability
governance_principles.copy: Identical inputs generate identical routes, scores, commitments, and terminal state.

governance_principles.title: Human legibility
governance_principles.copy: Every technical gate has a plain-language explanation and reviewer action.

threats.name: Validator capture
threats.control: Independent seats + dissent visibility + higher quorum

threats.name: Authority creep
threats.control: Explicit permission matrix + terminal human boundary

threats.name: Silent irreversible action
threats.control: No network, wallet, transaction, or settlement capability

claim_boundary: This is a deterministic browser-local digital twin and design reference, not a live node deployment.
No live ENS resolution, libp2p dialing, model call, API request, wallet connection, transaction, token movement, stake change, or settlement occurs.
Generated scores are simulated and do not certify factual correctness, security, legal compliance, economic value, or production readiness.
A validator quorum records simulated agreement; it is not an external audit or factual attestation.
The artifact chain is locally generated for inspectability and replay; it is not published to Ethereum or any external ledger.
GoalOS Mainnet deployment records displayed by the build are repository-derived and retain their own explicit production and authorization boundaries.
Every successful run terminates at HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; severe incidents terminate at SAFE_HOLD.
